release_body:
1. [新增] 为消息转发和群消息同步功能增加“地图”类消息支持
2. [调整] 为群消息同步功能调整了一些 emoji 表情 (用于代表转发后的头像)
3. [修复] 消息的对象的 `url` 属性未被反转义的问题
4. [调整] 临时将 `itchat` 版本依赖固定为 1.2.32，以避免近期发现的一些细节问题，期待修复

* Added ``/config`` endpoint that just outputs all configurations in JSON (usually for #50)
* Documented public upload facility (#50)
- Add utf-8 encoding to each python file. [Jose Diaz-Gonzalez]
remote_resolve=false fix

* Use UTF8 writer for stdout (#11) and regression test (@geggo).
* Minor testing fixes.
- cleanup

* Capability of enable/disable Travis for a repository.
* Option to synchronize with GitHub.

This is a major facelift of Numdifftools. Highlights for this release is:

  * Complex step method for derivative-order 1 through 14 is now available
  * Updated documentation

__Python 3 note__: Only the hello_world.py example and unit tests were run and do work fine. The pygtk and kivy examples may work, but were not tested.

__Changes in this Release__:
* Update to Chrome 53.0.2785.101 / CEF 3.2785.1481.gc3a7e2c.
* Python 3.4 / 3.5 support (#121, #183)
* Add image representation of drag & drop data in OSR (#251)
  * Update Kivy example
  * Add DragData.GetImage() and HasImage() methods, see API docs.
  * Add Image object and its methods to get image either as bitmap or png,
see API docs.

* Deprecated ntlm_auth.ntlm.Ntlm in favour of ntlm_auth.ntlm.NtlmContext
* This is because `Ntlm` is heavily geared towards HTTP auth which is not always the case, `NtlmContext` makes things more generic
* Updated docs and tests to reflect this
* Dropped support for Python 3.3

Changelog

- Replaced Tkinter clipboard functions with pyperclip cross-platform module
- Fixed issue with mouse move speed not respecting Settings
- Fixed issue with wheel() interpreting target patterns
- Fixed issue with freezing Lackey scripts into an executable
- Cleaned up PlatformManager documentation

- Allow input larger than 2GB
- Release the GIL on sufficiently large input
- Drop support for Python 3.2

---

Full list of changes: https://github.com/ifduyue/python-xxhash/compare/v1.0.1...v1.1.0

- AutoFocus API support.

- panwfapi.py: Add hash length to hash invalid length message.

- pan.wfapi: Use email package for HTTP header retrieval and parsing;
  also fixes potential issue with not handling a quoted filename in
  content-disposition header.

      content-disposition: attachment; filename="sample"

This is a beta (pre-release) package. It's still in development, but has
basic functionality fleshed out. Please refer to the `README` of the
project to get started.

- [PyPI - nistbeacon](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/django-letsencrypt)

## v0.9.1 (Beta Release)

- Made some minor fixes to the `README` file to work on Pypi
Fix release for AccordionHolder and TabHolder

** New **

* Support Django 1.8 to 1.11
* Fixed issues with cmsplugin-filer 1.1.

**Protocol**

Minor changes in the root URL (hello view):

- Added ``http_api_version`` (#600)
- Renamed ``hello`` to ``project_name``
- Renamed ``protocol_version`` to ``cliquet_protocol_version``
- Renamed ``documentation`` to ``project_docs``
- Renamed ``version`` to ``project_version``

**Breaking changes**

- When using *cliquet-fxa*, the setting ``multiauth.policy.fxa.use`` must now
  be explicitly set to ``cliquet_fxa.authentication.FxAOAuthAuthenticationPolicy``
- Fields in the root view were renamed (#600)

**Bug fixes**

- Include plugins after setting up components (like authn/authz) so that plugins
  can register views with permissions checking
- Remove ``__permissions__`` from impacted records values in ``ResourceChanged``
  events (#586)

**New features**

- New options in configuration of listeners to specify filtered actions and
  resource names (#492, #555)
- Add ability to listen to read action on resource (disabled by default)
  (#493)

**Internal**

- Fixed a few details in quickstart docs since backends are not Redis by default
  anymore
- Replace usage of ``assert`` by explicit exceptions since the former can
  be ignored when python is ran with ``-O`` (fixes #592)
- Improved documentation about permissions (#572, thanks for the feedback @MrChoclate)
- Fixed docs building under Python 3 (#591)

**Version control**

- **Cliquet 3.1.4**: https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/releases/tag/3.1.4
- **kinto 2.1.1**: https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/releases/tag/2.1.1
- **kinto-attachment 0.5.1**: https://github.com/Kinto/kinto-attachment/releases/tag/0.5.1
- **kinto-amo 0.1.1**: https://github.com/mozilla-services/kinto-amo/releases/tag/0.1.1
- **kinto-changes 0.2.0**: https://github.com/Kinto/kinto-changes/releases/tag/0.2.0
- **kinto-signer 0.5.0**: https://github.com/Kinto/kinto-signer/releases/tag/0.5.0
- **cliquet-fxa 1.4.0**: https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet-fxa/releases/tag/1.4.0
- **boto 2.40**: http://docs.pythonboto.org/en/latest/releasenotes/v2.40.0.html
* Fixed a bug where any passed params that were falsy would not be passed through to imgix.

v0.7.0 brings several new features, slight changes to default behavior, and some bug fixes and cleanup to Dactyl.

**Breaking Changes**

The default behavior when building HTML is now to copy static files into the output directory. This means the `-s` flag is usually not necessary anymore. If you _do not_ want to copy static files to the output directory, provide the `--no_static` (`-S`) flag instead.

There are also new flags for copying only content static (`-C`) or template static (`-T`) files.

**New Features**

Dactyl can now generate JSON formatted for upload to ElasticSearch using the `--es` flag, or even upload directly to ElasticSearch when building any type of content using the `--es_upload`. It only builds / uploads files that have an `md` source file. You can define custom templates for the JSON output. (See the [v0.7.0 README.md](https://github.com/ripple/dactyl/blob/v0.7.0/README.md) for details.)

**Bug Fixes and Cleanup**

- Filter imports are now compatible with Python 3.4 and lower ( #24 )
- Better error messaging for missing/broken filters and other issues ( #23 )
- Correctly outputs the version number or help statement even if the config file is invalid ( #22 )
- When using `--bypass_errors`, a preprocessing error falls back to simply parsing the Markdown content as-is instead of resulting in a blank page.
- Starts refactoring the source files to split up functionality better into smaller pieces for easier reusability.
- Unifies logging and output controls across `dactyl_build`, `dactyl_link_checker`, and `dactyl_style_checker`

- Attach lun and modify hlu if it's 0.
- Detach the dummy lun which was attached via legacy storops.
- [Feature] #29 : Expose compression support from Paramiko (inherited from SSH).

Features:
-  Support playback with binary and large response payload #38
Bump Toil to 3.1.4 on toil-box.

**This package is now available on PyPI!**

```bash
$ pip install ciscosparkapi
```

v0.1.1 - Fixed an issue where the API sub-package (ciscosparkapi.api) wasn't included in the source distribution package.

* system/tmos API added
* authz/tokens API added
* additional handling of descriptions for policy rules. This attribute did not exist before a specific version of BIG-IP, so the SDK will now emit a warning if you attempt to use it. It will then fix up your request to not include it and forward the request on to BIG-IP

This is a minor bugfix release.

It includes:

* logging upgrades
* removal of a debug prinout
* doc enhancements
* dependency constraint on requests >= 2.9.1

**CHANGES**
 - Now using Travis CI (deployments on tagged releases, and auto-generation of docs)
 - Unit/integration tests now passing
 - Improvements to methods related to graph features
 - Flexible requirements

**NOTE**: the changes to graph feature methods are technically breaking, but these features were pre-released with development API, so a major version bump is not necessary.
Dependency updates and fix to use most recent VAPID calls.

# Changes
- Support for `index_predicate` in all shortcut methods and bulk upsert (PR #49)
